# Wardley.app

Wardley.app is a Phoenix web application for creating and editing Wardley Maps.

## LLM Integration

Repository guide:

- LLM_INTEGRATION.md

Use the REST API for live browser updates. The browser editor loads `GET /api/map` and subscribes to the active map through Phoenix Channels, so edits made through the running Phoenix app are reflected in the open map.

Default local base URL:

- http://localhost:4000

## Auth

Local development API access is currently unauthenticated. Only trusted local callers should use write endpoints. Do not invent credentials. If deployment adds auth, use an out-of-band bearer token and never place secrets in prompts, node text, metadata, URLs, or logs.

## Key Endpoints

- `GET /api/map` - active/default map for the editor.
- `GET /api/maps` - list maps.
- `GET /api/maps/:id` - map JSON with nodes and edges.
- `GET /api/maps/:id/dsl` - compact OWM DSL.
- `GET /api/maps/:id/svg` - rendered SVG.
- `POST /api/nodes` - create a node.
- `PATCH /api/nodes/:id` - update a node.
- `DELETE /api/nodes/:id` - delete a node.
- `POST /api/edges` - create a dependency edge.
- `PATCH /api/edges/:id` - update edge metadata.
- `DELETE /api/edges/:id` - delete an edge.

## Coordinates

REST uses percentages:

- `x_pct`: evolution, 0 genesis to 100 commodity.
- `y_pct`: visibility, 0 most visible to 100 least visible.

OWM DSL uses `[visibility, evolution]` from 0.0 to 1.0.

## Map Rules

- Start with user needs.
- Edges mean `source` depends on `target`.
- Value chain is dependency structure, not process sequence.
- Preserve assumptions and uncertainty.
- Ask before broad destructive edits.
